channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: Iran Military Claims Drone Strikes On US Bases In Kuwait, Jordan, Bahrain

media:content.media:description: There was no confirmation from the US of targeting of its facilities or personnel. (Representational)

item.description: Tehran argues that any facility or base in the Middle East that the US military uses for strikes on Iran is considered a legitimate target for retaliation.
item.content:encoded: Tehran argues that any facility or base in the Middle East that the US military uses for strikes on Iran is considered a legitimate target for retaliation.

item.title: Spain Declares National Emergency Over Wildfires Amid Europe Heatwave

media:content.media:description: Spain last declared this emergency level during last year's nationwide power blackout.

item.description: French authorities ordered the total evacuation of nealy 40,00 people in the Cap Ferret peninsula, while more than 10,000 people were evacuated from the west of Madrid, Spain.
item.content:encoded: French authorities ordered the total evacuation of nealy 40,00 people in the Cap Ferret peninsula, while more than 10,000 people were evacuated from the west of Madrid, Spain.

item.title: Bangladesh President Mohammed Shahabuddin Resigns Citing Health Issues

media:content.media:description: The resignation of Shahabuddin would leave Hasina with no allies in high office.

item.description: Shahabuddin, 76, has held the largely ceremonial post of president since April 2023, and is a former ally of ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.
item.content:encoded: Shahabuddin, 76, has held the largely ceremonial post of president since April 2023, and is a former ally of ousted Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina.

item.title: 50,000 Flee As Wildfires Ravage France, Spain

media:content.media:description: The total area of land burnt so far this year in EU countries is the second largest

item.description: In Spain, the government declared a state of emergency in the Madrid region, where more than 10,000 people have been evacuated in recent days.
item.content:encoded: In Spain, the government declared a state of emergency in the Madrid region, where more than 10,000 people have been evacuated in recent days.

item.title: Opinion: Opinion | 11 Years Later, Houthis Have Returned To Finish Unfinished Business With Pakistan
item.description: Pakistan has been here before, and the last time, it barely escaped.
item.content:encoded: Pakistan has been here before, and the last time, it barely escaped.

item.title: Alcohol, Cannabis, Xanax: Saudi Prince Died At London Hotel After Taking Cocktail Of Drugs

media:content.media:description: The prince checked into the London hotel on November 19 for a week-long stay

item.description: A cleaner found the Saudi royal lying on the bathroom floor of his locked fifth-floor room, fully clothed. Paramedics were rushed but were unable to resuscitate him.
item.content:encoded: A cleaner found the Saudi royal lying on the bathroom floor of his locked fifth-floor room, fully clothed. Paramedics were rushed but were unable to resuscitate him.

item.title: AI 'Kill Switch' Bill Floated by US House Lawmakers
item.description: The lawmakers announced the legislation days after OpenAI said its AI agent went rogue
item.content:encoded: The lawmakers announced the legislation days after OpenAI said its AI agent went rogue

item.title: Philippines Accuses China Of Water Cannon Use As Clashes Continue For 2nd Day

media:content.media:description: A Chinese coast guard ship fires a water cannon at a Philippine vessel in South China Sea

item.description: "The PCG strongly condemns the dangerous and unprofessional actions of the China Coast Guard," saysspokesperson Jay Tarriela.
item.content:encoded: "The PCG strongly condemns the dangerous and unprofessional actions of the China Coast Guard," saysspokesperson Jay Tarriela.

item.title: Diet Coke Prices Jump Over 10% In India As Iran War Hits Supply Chain

media:content.media:description: There are fears the disruption could widen further to block another sea route.

item.description: Coke's hike is an example of how the Middle East conflict is still forcing global companies to alter their supply chains and increase prices in their consumer markets.
item.content:encoded: Coke's hike is an example of how the Middle East conflict is still forcing global companies to alter their supply chains and increase prices in their consumer markets.

item.title: Pak's Asim Munir Used A Private Oil Deal In Iran To Shield Saudi: Report

media:content.media:description: Pakistan has played the mediator between Iran and the United States in the war

item.description: Asim Munir mediated the Saudi-Iran deal by dangling the continued operation of a joint oil transport venture with the IRGC Chief, which has been quietly transporting oil from Iran to Pakistan since late last year.
item.content:encoded: Asim Munir mediated the Saudi-Iran deal by dangling the continued operation of a joint oil transport venture with the IRGC Chief, which has been quietly transporting oil from Iran to Pakistan since late last year.

item.title: Trump Station In New York? $8 Billion Penn Station Revamp Fuels Renaming Buzz

media:content.media:description: Controversy grows over potential Trump state on Penn State renovation.

item.description: The Republican administration this week urged leading US Senators to rename Penn Station as it undertakes a projected $8 billion redesign of the underground Manhattan transit hub.
item.content:encoded: The Republican administration this week urged leading US Senators to rename Penn Station as it undertakes a projected $8 billion redesign of the underground Manhattan transit hub.

item.title: Scientists Capture Orcas Smashing Giant Sunfish Into Thousands Of Pieces

media:content.media:description: The footage shows a female orca holding the tail fin of a recently killed sunfish.

item.description: Scientists captured orcas in the Gulf of California smashing a giant sunfish into tiny pieces to feed group members.
item.content:encoded: Scientists captured orcas in the Gulf of California smashing a giant sunfish into tiny pieces to feed group members.

item.title: Iran-US War Live: US Strikes Iran Again, Bahrain Warns Of Incoming Fire As War Escalates
item.description: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in Hormuz, drawing an "eye for an eye" warning from Tehran as both sides sharpened threats in the escalating war.
item.content:encoded: US-Iran War News Live Updates: US President Donald Trump had threatened to destroy an Iranian bridge or power plant for every attack on shipping in Hormuz, drawing an "eye for an eye" warning from Tehran as both sides sharpened threats in the escalating war.

item.title: Saudi Has Trillions In Oil Wealth, But Here's Why It Wants Nuclear Power

media:content.media:description: Saudi Arabia has said its nuclear programme is for civilian purposes.

item.description: Even though Saudi Arabia has a lot of oil and other natural resources, the government has recorded budget deficits in nine of the last 10 years.
item.content:encoded: Even though Saudi Arabia has a lot of oil and other natural resources, the government has recorded budget deficits in nine of the last 10 years.

item.title: No Diwali Holiday In California As Court Strikes Down Governor Gavin's Order

media:content.media:description: California boasts the highest population of Indian Americans in the country.

item.description: The law did not make Diwali a mandatory paid holiday for everyone. It allowed eligible state employees to use paid leave and gave public schools and community colleges the option to close in observance of the festival.
item.content:encoded: The law did not make Diwali a mandatory paid holiday for everyone. It allowed eligible state employees to use paid leave and gave public schools and community colleges the option to close in observance of the festival.

item.title: Over 30,000 Evacuated As Wildfires Burn Through France And Spain

media:content.media:description: Three firefighters killed as severe heat and 40C+ temperatures hit southern Europe.

item.description: The total area of land burnt so far this year in EU countries is the second largest on record, reported by satellite data from the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS).
item.content:encoded: The total area of land burnt so far this year in EU countries is the second largest on record, reported by satellite data from the European Forest Fire Information System (EFFIS).

item.title: Nepal Aid Workers Turn To Sex Work After Donald Trump's USAID Cuts

media:content.media:description: In impoverished Nepal, the impact on aid workers' lives was particularly crushing

item.description: Of the more than 281,000 jobs lost due to Trump's dismantling of USAID, just 23,000 were American, says Sadie Healy, who runs a global health consulting firm and has been tracking the job losses worldwide.
item.content:encoded: Of the more than 281,000 jobs lost due to Trump's dismantling of USAID, just 23,000 were American, says Sadie Healy, who runs a global health consulting firm and has been tracking the job losses worldwide.

item.title: US National, Khalistani Extremist Among 5 Caught Entering India Illegally From Nepal

media:content.media:description: The Punjab Police has been looking for both men in separate cases

item.description: Three Indian nationals -- Suresh Kumar Pathak, Dilip Upadhyay, both residents of Rupaidiha town, and vehicle driver Rahul Kumar -- were also arrested in a joint operation by the Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) and the Uttar Pradesh Police.
item.content:encoded: Three Indian nationals -- Suresh Kumar Pathak, Dilip Upadhyay, both residents of Rupaidiha town, and vehicle driver Rahul Kumar -- were also arrested in a joint operation by the Sashastra Seema Bal (SSB) and the Uttar Pradesh Police.

item.title: Indian Man's Body Found In Israeli Flat In Pool Of Blood

media:content.media:description: The exact cause of death remains unclear, the police said.

item.description: The investigation indicates a criminal background, and a 31-year-old man from the southern city of Eilat has been arrested on suspicion of involvement in the murder.
item.content:encoded: The investigation indicates a criminal background, and a 31-year-old man from the southern city of Eilat has been arrested on suspicion of involvement in the murder.

item.title: "We Don't Need To Respond In Advance": Carney On Trump's 50% Tariff Threat

media:content.media:description: Carney said the tariff threat could be a negotiation tactic by the US.

item.description: The new tariffs cover a wide range of goods, including honey, liquor, cement, dairy products, some wood products, hockey sticks and other items.
item.content:encoded: The new tariffs cover a wide range of goods, including honey, liquor, cement, dairy products, some wood products, hockey sticks and other items.
